payload.diagram_title: I just heard a colleague say "AI agents will make apps

post_text:
📌 Agentic AI Decision Tree

I just heard a colleague say "AI agents will make apps obsolete" and I have to strongly disagree. As a Staff Engineer, I think Nothing CEO Carl Pei's vision of AI agents replacing apps is oversimplified. I'm not convinced that AI can fully understand the complexity of human intent, at least not with our current technology.

Let's compare the capabilities of AI agents and traditional apps:
```
 Traditional Apps → Fine-grained control over user experience
 AI Agents → Limited control, relying on machine learning models
 Traditional Apps → Direct user feedback and iteration
 AI Agents → Indirect feedback, relying on data analysis
```
The flow of decision-making with AI agents is also more complex:
```
🟦 User Input │
▼
🟩 Natural Language Processing │
▼
🟨 Machine Learning Model │
▼
🟥 Action Taken
```
I think Nvidia's quiet success in the networking business, with $11 billion in revenue last quarter, is a more interesting story. It shows that there's still a lot of value in traditional technology. I'm not ready to write off apps just yet.
FBI's actions, on the other hand, raise serious concerns about privacy and surveillance.
I think we need to be cautious about the implications of AI agents and data collection.
💬 Can we really trust AI agents to make decisions on our behalf, and what are the consequences of relying on commercially available location data? #AIagents #SmartphoneApps #Nvidia #FBI #LocationData #PrivacyMatters #TechnologicalEthics
